references.title: Adapting Bayesian Knowledge Tracing to a Massive Open Online Course in edX

extracted.Student Performance Definition: Next Question is Correct on First Attempt
extracted.Flaw of Previous Models:
In intelligent tutoring systems there is a connection of the Knowledge Components associated with each Question in the System.

Getting experts to map questions to knowledge components is not trivial in a MOOC context. Moreover, in a MOOC setting, KC are often treated in isolation, leaving little chance to see evolution of KCs over time.

MOOCs also have a different form of accepting an answer as correct and /or incorrect - ITS use first answer, MOOC often consider last attempt.
extracted.Novelty of Model:
The authors’ implementation of BKT includes a variant that allows the observation of multiple subparts at once (IDEM). 

It also includes a parameter to measure the number of attempts needed to get the correct answer (Count).
extracted.Preprocessing Details: Logs were separated by user and the representation of each user is the sequence of activities associated with a problem.
extracted.Comments:
Fitting of BKT model was done via Expectation-Maximization. 

Models are divided into types of materials. Authors analyze the results in terms of problem types: Homework problems, Lccture Problems and finally Exam Problems.

The model is percurssor for KT in MOOCs with 2 that address 2 challenges:

1. Lack of a KC model for MOOCs,

2. Unpenalized Attempts Counts
extracted.Limitations:
Authors only publish results using AUC score.

Comparison with ASSISTments scores is soewhat unnecessary as interpretation of contexts is substantially different.

Subpart separation is not ideal to predict success throughut the course.

A single dataset and course.

No extension to DKT framework, focusing only in BKT.

annotations.text: MOOCs are becoming more and more prevalent in higher education. However, this proliferation follows the traditional educational paradigm in many regards. The paper shows tha BKT can be adapted to a course on edX. The experiment tests BKTs success on 3 distinct challenges: lack of knowledge component models, unpenalized multiple attempts for quizzes/tests and diverging learning pathways through the system.
